article_meta.title: Takeaways from the millions of newly released Epstein files 2 hours agoShareSaveSakshi VenkatramanandKwasi Gyamfi Asiedu

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for an article that contains several defensible instances of author-level bias. While the article is largely factual reporting, there are notable instances of loaded language and framing choices in the author's own prose that could be flagged. For example: 'putting more holes in Andrew's assurances' is editorializing by the author (Spin/Word Choice); 'controversial plea agreement' uses a subjective qualifying adjective; the structural framing of certain sections shows slant; 'disgraced financier' is a subjective qualifying adjective applied by the author. However, the article is also largely a news summary with heavy use of attributed statements, so some restraint is understandable. The model correctly avoided flagging any quoted text, which is good, but missed several author-prose bias instances.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero attribution rule violations. No quoted text was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged at all, so there are zero false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several defensible author-level bias instances: (1) 'disgraced financier' - Subjective Qualifying Adjective used repeatedly by the author; (2) 'putting more holes in Andrew's assurances' - Spin/editorial commentary by the author interpreting the significance of the emails; (3) 'controversial plea agreement' - Subjective Qualifying Adjective; (4) the structural framing around the DOJ's treatment of Trump-related allegations, where the author gives significant space to DOJ's defense of Trump without equally contextualizing other figures' denials. These are moderate bias instances - some are arguable - but returning a completely empty array misses legitimate concerns.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No bias types were applied since the output was empty. Cannot evaluate accuracy of labels that don't exist, so scoring neutrally at 1.
